functions.name: addEFMigrations
functions.description: Adds EF Core migration management for polyglot app hosts.

functions.name: bundleBaseImage
functions.description: Gets or sets the base container image for the migration bundle container.
functions.remarks:
When set, this overrides the default base image selection entirely. Use this when the
default image (derived from the project's target framework) isn't suitable — for example
when targeting a preview SDK or a custom base image with extra dependencies.
Example: `"mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime:11.0-preview"`.

functions.name: bundleSelfContained
functions.description: Gets or sets whether the migration bundle should be self-contained.

functions.name: bundleTargetRuntime
functions.description: Gets or sets the target runtime identifier for the migration bundle (e.g., "linux-x64", "win-x64").

functions.name: dbContextTypeName
functions.description: Gets the fully qualified name of the DbContext type to use for migrations, or null to auto-detect.
functions.remarks:
This property is used to specify which DbContext to use when the project contains multiple DbContext types.
When null, the EF Core tools will auto-detect the DbContext to use.

functions.name: migrationNamespace
functions.description: Gets or sets the namespace for new migrations. Used by the Add Migration command.
functions.remarks: If not specified, the namespace will be derived from the project's default namespace.

functions.name: migrationOutputDirectory
functions.description: Gets or sets the output directory for new migrations. Used by the Add Migration command.
functions.remarks: If not specified, migrations will be placed in the default 'Migrations' directory.

functions.name: migrationsProjectPath
functions.description: Gets or sets the path to the project containing the migrations, when it's not the same as the startup project.
functions.remarks:
If not specified, migrations are assumed to be in the startup project.
When specified, this project's path will be used as the target for migration operations.

functions.name: projectResource
functions.description: Gets the parent project resource that contains the DbContext.

functions.name: publishBundleContainer
functions.description: Gets or sets whether the migration bundle should be published as a container image that applies the migrations to the database at deploy time.
functions.remarks:
When `true`, the resource is materialized as a `ContainerResource`
carrying a generated `Dockerfile` that wraps the migration bundle. The compute environment
(Docker Compose, Azure Container Apps, Kubernetes, Azure App Service, Azure Functions, etc.)
deploys it alongside the other compute resources and supplies the connection string from any
`IResourceWithConnectionString` dependency declared via `WithReference` or
`WaitFor`.

functions.name: scriptIdempotent
functions.description: Gets or sets whether the migration script should be idempotent (include IF NOT EXISTS checks).

functions.name: scriptNoTransactions
functions.description: Gets or sets whether to omit transaction statements from the migration script.

functions.name: setBundleBaseImage
functions.description: Gets or sets the base container image for the migration bundle container.
functions.remarks:
When set, this overrides the default base image selection entirely. Use this when the
default image (derived from the project's target framework) isn't suitable — for example
when targeting a preview SDK or a custom base image with extra dependencies.
Example: `"mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime:11.0-preview"`.

functions.name: setBundleSelfContained
functions.description: Gets or sets whether the migration bundle should be self-contained.

functions.name: setBundleTargetRuntime
functions.description: Gets or sets the target runtime identifier for the migration bundle (e.g., "linux-x64", "win-x64").

functions.name: setMigrationNamespace
functions.description: Gets or sets the namespace for new migrations. Used by the Add Migration command.
functions.remarks: If not specified, the namespace will be derived from the project's default namespace.

functions.name: setMigrationOutputDirectory
functions.description: Gets or sets the output directory for new migrations. Used by the Add Migration command.
functions.remarks: If not specified, migrations will be placed in the default 'Migrations' directory.

functions.name: setMigrationsProjectPath
functions.description: Gets or sets the path to the project containing the migrations, when it's not the same as the startup project.
functions.remarks:
If not specified, migrations are assumed to be in the startup project.
When specified, this project's path will be used as the target for migration operations.

functions.name: setPublishBundleContainer
functions.description: Gets or sets whether the migration bundle should be published as a container image that applies the migrations to the database at deploy time.
functions.remarks:
When `true`, the resource is materialized as a `ContainerResource`
carrying a generated `Dockerfile` that wraps the migration bundle. The compute environment
(Docker Compose, Azure Container Apps, Kubernetes, Azure App Service, Azure Functions, etc.)
deploys it alongside the other compute resources and supplies the connection string from any
`IResourceWithConnectionString` dependency declared via `WithReference` or
`WaitFor`.

functions.name: setScriptIdempotent
functions.description: Gets or sets whether the migration script should be idempotent (include IF NOT EXISTS checks).

functions.name: setScriptNoTransactions
functions.description: Gets or sets whether to omit transaction statements from the migration script.

functions.name: publishAsMigrationBundle
functions.description: Configures the EF migration resource to generate a migration bundle during publishing.
functions.remarks:
During `aspire publish`, the bundle executable is written to the publish output directory
under the `efmigrations` folder. When `publishContainer` is
`true`, Aspire also generates a `Dockerfile` that packages the bundle into
a container image; the container reads the connection string from a
`ConnectionStrings__<name>` environment variable injected automatically for a
`IResourceWithConnectionString` that the migration resource references or waits on.
The startup project (the project on which `AddEFMigrations` was invoked) and the
migrations project (configured via `WithMigrationsProject`
or `WithMigrationsProject``1`, if
different) must both list the target runtime in their `<RuntimeIdentifiers>` MSBuild property.
If `publishContainer` = `true`, by default this means
adding at minimum `<RuntimeIdentifiers>linux-x64</RuntimeIdentifiers>` to
both projects.
functions.signature: publishAsMigrationBundle(targetRuntime?: string, selfContained?: boolean, publishContainer?: boolean, baseImage?: string): EFMigrationResource

parameters.name: targetRuntime
parameters.description: The target runtime identifier for the bundle (e.g., `linux-x64`, `win-x64`). If `null` and `publishContainer` is `true`, defaults to `linux-x64` to match the default Linux base container image used for the generated `Dockerfile`. If `null` and `publishContainer` is `false`, the bundle targets the runtime hosting `aspire publish`.

parameters.name: selfContained
parameters.description: If `true`, creates a self-contained bundle that includes the .NET runtime.

parameters.name: publishContainer
parameters.description: If `true`, the bundle is published as a container image that applies migrations at deploy time. The resource becomes a compute resource; each target environment deploys it the same way it deploys any other container (supplying connection strings from referenced `IResourceWithConnectionString` dependencies via the standard `WithReference` mechanism).

parameters.name: baseImage
parameters.description: Overrides the base container image for the generated `Dockerfile`. When `null` (the default), the image is derived from the project's target framework — for example, `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime:10.0` for a `net10.0` framework-dependent bundle. Set this when the default is not suitable, e.g. for preview SDKs or custom base images. Only meaningful when `publishContainer` is `true`.

functions.name: publishAsMigrationScript
functions.description: Configures the EF migration resource to generate a migration script during publishing.
functions.remarks:
During `aspire publish`, the generated SQL script is written to the publish output directory under
the `efmigrations` folder. The script is included as a deployment artifact, but it is not executed
automatically during deployment.

parameters.name: idempotent
parameters.description: If `true` (the default), generates an idempotent script with `IF NOT EXISTS` checks so it can be safely re-run against a database that has already had some or all of the migrations applied.

parameters.name: noTransactions
parameters.description: If `true`, omits transaction statements from the script.

functions.name: runDatabaseUpdateOnStart
functions.description: Configures the EF migration resource to run database update when the AppHost starts.
functions.remarks:
When enabled, migrations are applied during AppHost startup. This only affects local
run-mode execution. The migrations resource is not deployed with the app, so this method
has no effect during publish or deployment.
A health check is automatically registered for this resource, allowing other resources to use
`.WaitFor()` to wait until migrations complete before starting.

functions.name: withMigrationNamespace
functions.description: Configures the namespace for new migrations created with the Add Migration command.
functions.remarks:
If not specified, the namespace will be derived from the project's default namespace.
Example: `MyApp.Data.Migrations` or `MyApp.Infrastructure.Migrations`.

functions.name: withMigrationOutputDirectory
functions.description: Configures the output directory for new migrations created with the Add Migration command.
functions.remarks:
If not specified, migrations will be placed in the default `Migrations` directory.
Example: `Data/Migrations` or `Infrastructure/Migrations`.

parameters.name: outputDirectory
parameters.description: The output directory path relative to the project root.

functions.name: withMigrationsProject
functions.description: Configures a separate project containing migrations for polyglot app hosts.

handleTypes.name: EFMigrationResource
handleTypes.description: Represents an EF Core migration resource associated with a project.
